sections.title: Dual-Level Job Structure
sections.content: For intermediate goods like guide rails, the JTBD framework operates at two levels: the **specification job** (engineering) and the **procurement job** (purchasing). Both must be satisfied for market entry. [SRC: Burleson JTBD framework]

sections.title: 5-Level Job Pyramid
sections.content:
| Level | Job Type | Statement | Owner |
|-------|----------|-----------|-------|
| L1 (Aspirational) | Life goal | "Ensure safe, reliable vertical transportation for building occupants" | Building owner / operator |
| L2 (Strategic) | Strategic outcome | "Install and maintain elevator systems that meet safety codes with minimal lifecycle cost" | Elevator OEM (Schindler, KONE, Otis, TK Elevator) |
| L3 (Core functional) | Core job | "Specify and procure guide rails that guarantee car guidance accuracy under all load and speed conditions" | Elevator system engineer |
| L4 (Related functional) | Support job | "Source guide rail material that arrives on time, meets EN 81-1 specs, and minimizes total installed cost" | Procurement / supply chain manager |
| L5 (Emotional/social) | Emotional job | "Confidence that the guide rail supplier is reliable and will not cause project delays or safety incidents" | Project manager / quality director |

sections.title: Core Job (L3) Decomposition — 8-Step Job Map
sections.content:
| Step | Job Step | Description | Importance (1-10) | Current Satisfaction (1-10) | Gap |
|------|----------|-------------|-------------------|---------------------------|-----|
| 1 | Define requirements | Determine rail profile, material grade, hardness from elevator speed, capacity, and shaft layout | 9 | 7 | 2 |
| 2 | Locate suppliers | Find qualified guide rail manufacturers with EN 81-1 / EN 81-50 certification | 8 | 6 | 2 |
| 3 | Evaluate samples | Test dimensional accuracy, hardness, straightness, surface quality of sample rails | 9 | 5 | 4 |
| 4 | Negotiate terms | Agree pricing, MOQ, lead time, quality guarantees, tooling amortization | 7 | 6 | 1 |
| 5 | Order & schedule | Place orders timed to building construction schedule (rail installation = early phase) | 8 | 5 | 3 |
| 6 | Receive & inspect | Incoming inspection: dimensional check, hardness test, straightness measurement | 9 | 6 | 3 |
| 7 | Install in shaft | Mount guide rail sections in hoistway, align with plumb laser, torque fishplate bolts | 10 | 7 | 3 |
| 8 | Validate & certify | Load test, ride quality test, TUeV or notified body sign-off | 10 | 8 | 2 |

sections.title: Underserved Outcomes (Gap >= 3)
sections.content:
| Step | Outcome | Importance | Satisfaction | Opportunity Score | Category |
|------|---------|-----------|-------------|------------------|----------|
| 3 | Minimize variation between sample and production rails | 9 | 5 | 13 | UNDERSERVED |
| 5 | Minimize lead time from order to delivery for repeat orders | 8 | 5 | 11 | UNDERSERVED |
| 6 | Minimize effort required for incoming dimensional inspection | 9 | 6 | 12 | UNDERSERVED |
| 7 | Minimize time to align guide rail sections in hoistway | 10 | 7 | 13 | UNDERSERVED |

**Opportunity algorithm**: Importance + (Importance - Satisfaction) = Opportunity Score. Scores >= 10 indicate underserved outcomes. [SRC: Ulwick ODI methodology]

sections.title: Specification Job — Key Requirements
sections.content:
| Requirement | Standard | Typical Spec | Tolerance | Criticality |
|-------------|----------|-------------|-----------|-------------|
| Rail profile geometry | ISO 7465 | T89/B (89mm web) | +0.5/-0 mm web height | CRITICAL — car shoe clearance |
| Rail head straightness | EN 81-50 | 0.3 mm/m max | Measured over 1m gauge | CRITICAL — ride quality |
| Running surface hardness | OEM spec | 50-55 HRC (induction) | +/-3 HRC | HIGH — shoe wear life |
| Running surface roughness | OEM spec | Ra 1.6-3.2 um | As rolled/ground | MEDIUM — lubrication |
| Joint face flatness | EN 81-1 Annex G | 0.05 mm max | Measured across joint | CRITICAL — continuity |
| Length tolerance | ISO 7465 | 5,000 +/-1 mm | Per bar | HIGH — installation fit |
| Material cert | EN 10204 | Type 3.1 | - | MANDATORY |

sections.title: Procurement Job — Decision Criteria
sections.content:
| Criterion | Weight | Current Best | Pain Point |
|-----------|--------|-------------|------------|
| Unit price (EUR/m) | 25% | Savera (scale advantage) | Asian imports at 15-20% discount but quality risk |
| Delivery reliability (% OTIF) | 20% | VOL-Stahl (95%+) | Savera sometimes 8-10 week delays on peak demand |
| Quality consistency (reject rate) | 20% | Montanstahl (<1%) | Some suppliers 3-5% reject on straightness |
| Lead time (weeks, repeat orders) | 15% | 4-6 weeks | New profile sizes: 10-14 weeks |
| Technical support / engineering | 10% | Montanstahl | Most suppliers reactive, not proactive |
| Payment terms | 5% | Net 60 standard | - |
| Sustainability / CO2 footprint | 5% | Emerging requirement | No supplier differentiated yet |

job_pyramid.statement: Ensure safe, reliable vertical transportation for building occupants
Install and maintain elevator systems that meet safety codes with minimal lifecycle cost
Specify and procure guide rails that guarantee car guidance accuracy under all load and speed conditions
Source guide rail material that arrives on time, meets EN 81-1 specs, and minimizes total installed cost
Confidence in supplier reliability and safety performance
